text: The procedure is performed in the right lateral decubitus position with carbon dioxide insufflation to a pressure of six millimeters of mercury.
The eventration is easily identified along with the stronger diaphragmatic rim on each side.
All proline sutures take big bites of the diaphragm on each side of the eventration.
Each placating suture approximates the diaphragmatic margins, pushing the weakened component inferiorly.
The suture is tied extracorporeally and cut long.
The second suture is placed approximately one centimeter away from the first.
A clip is placed on the tied suture to allow the placation site to be identified on a chest x-ray.
The placation continues from lateral to medial.
